summary: Matched the common fighter jab/Attack1 path by moving the Attack11/12/13 logic out of the large ft_08A4 assembly blob into new ftCommon C sources and headers, while retaining several hard-to-match adjacent helpers as MWERKS inline asm in ftCo_08A6.c. The PR also updated motion-state wiring, renamed the old ft_8008A9F8 entry point to ftCo_Attack1_DecideMotion at callsites, added attack1 motion vars, and tightened related fighter/item types.
what_changed: Deleted roughly 976 lines from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced the Attack1/Jab region with new C sources under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack1.c implementing ftCo_Attack1_DecideMotion plus Attack11, Attack12, and Attack13 Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll-related behavior.
Renamed and propagated the former ft_8008A9F8 attack-decision routine as ftCo_Attack1_DecideMotion in wait, squat, walk, turn, landing, appeal, Game & Watch, Samus, and other IASA call paths.
Added ftCo_Attack1.h for the public Attack1/Jab callbacks and removed those declarations from the broader ft_0877.h header.

Added ftCo_08A6.c and ftCo_08A6.h to hold still-assembly-backed helpers around 8008A698, 8008A6D8, and ft_8008A7A8, plus declarations for ftCo_Attack_800CCF58, ftCo_Attack_800CDD14, and ftCo_Attack_800D6A50.
Updated ftCo_Init.c motion-state table so Attack11, Attack12, and Attack13 use ftCo_Attack11_Phys and ftCo_Attack11_Coll instead of the older ftCo_Attack1_Phys/Coll names.
Added ftCommon motion vars struct ftCo_Attack1Vars with bool x0 at fp+2340 for jab chaining state.
Renamed Fighter fields in src/melee/ft/types.h: x196C_hitlag_mult became hitlag_mul, x1970 became unk_msid, and item object fields were typed as Item_GObj*.
Adjusted ftCo_DatAttrs float fields from f32 to float and changed x190_Jab_3InputWindow from s32 to float in the struct definition.
Flattened Fighter x2218 bitfields out of an anonymous union while preserving the named x2218_b0 through x2218_b7 flags.
Added src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.h for extern string symbols ftWaitAnim_803C54A8 and ftWaitAnim_803C54C4 used by the retained assembly helper.
Changed it_8026B30C to take Item_GObj*, return enum_t, use GET_ITEM, and removed a duplicate header declaration.
Updated obj_files.mk to build ftCo_08A6.c.o and ftCo_Attack1.c.o.
smart_moves: Split the matched high-level Attack1 state logic from still-unmatched low-level helper code: ftCo_Attack1.c is readable C, while ftCo_08A6.c preserves exact MWERKS inline asm for difficult nearby routines.
Introduced a semantically meaningful public name, ftCo_Attack1_DecideMotion, for a heavily reused IASA input decision routine instead of keeping the address-style ft_8008A9F8 name.
Moved Attack1 declarations into focused ftCommon headers, reducing dependency on the catch-all ft_0877.h.
Kept address comments in headers and static prototypes, preserving searchability and mapping back to DOL addresses while improving names.
Used typed Item_GObj* for item-related fighter fields and it_8026B30C, making item handling in Attack1 decision logic clearer and less dependent on raw HSD_GObj pointers.
Preserved exact matching artifacts where needed, including MWERKS_GEKKO guards, nofralloc inline asm, local lbl_ labels, and MUST_MATCH unused stack padding comments.
decomp_lessons: For large monolithic asm files, it is useful to carve out a coherent gameplay feature into a named C file while leaving adjacent problematic routines as inline asm in a companion file.
A common action decision routine should be renamed and propagated across all IASA callsites once its purpose is known; this makes future control-flow review far easier.
Motion-state callbacks should be named for the concrete state they serve when possible; this PR changed generic Attack1 Phys/Coll names to Attack11 Phys/Coll callbacks used by Attack11/12/13 entries.
Struct field naming can unlock decomp readability: hitlag_mul, unk_msid, item_gobj, and mv.co.attack1.x0 make the jab input-window code much easier to reason about than raw x196C/x1970 offsets.
Do not force all nearby assembly into C at once. Keeping hard cases as inline asm behind MWERKS_GEKKO can still allow the main feature file to match and be integrated.
When an assembly helper references data symbols from another module, add a narrow header for those externs rather than leaking them through unrelated headers.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use the ftCo_ prefix and action name, e.g. ftCo_Attack1.c and ftCo_Attack1.h.
Recovered decision/check helpers were named by role: ftCo_Attack1_DecideMotion, checkAttack11, checkAttack12, checkAttack13, doAttack12, doAttack13.
Unmatched or unclear address-based helpers retained address names such as lbl_8008A698, lbl_8008A6D8, and ft_8008A7A8.
Public attack callbacks follow state suffixes: ftCo_Attack11_Anim, ftCo_Attack11_IASA, ftCo_Attack11_Phys, ftCo_Attack11_Coll, ftCo_Attack12_Anim, ftCo_Attack13_IASA.
Header guards continue the address-based GALE01 convention, e.g. GALE01_08A9F8 and GALE01_08A698.
Fighter struct fields moved from raw offset names toward behavior names where evidence was clear, such as hitlag_mul and item_gobj; uncertain state id remained conservative as unk_msid.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm with #pragma push/pop and nofralloc to preserve exact codegen for lbl_8008A698, lbl_8008A6D8, and ft_8008A7A8.
Converted local assembly labels from .L_8008xxxx to C-compatible lbl_8008xxxx where branches crossed into inline asm.
Defined local constants under MWERKS_GEKKO and !WIP, including lbl_804D8448, lbl_804D844C, ftCo_804D8450, and lbl_804D3AE0, to satisfy inline asm references.
Inserted MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays in C functions where the matched code required stack layout artifacts.
Kept address comments beside function declarations and static prototypes to support verification against the original binary.
Updated remaining asm callsites in ft_08A4.s and ft_0877.s to branch to the new C/inline-asm symbol names without changing branch distances semantically.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context, so no explicit reviewer feedback is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile ftCo_08A6.c inline asm helpers lbl_8008A698, lbl_8008A6D8, or ft_8008A7A8 into C.
Find follow-up renames for Fighter::unk_msid and mv.co.attack1.x0 once their semantics became clearer.
Search for later cleanup around ftCo_Attack_800CCF58, ftCo_Attack_800CDD14, and ftCo_Attack_800D6A50.
Check whether x190_Jab_3InputWindow remaining as float was confirmed by data usage in subsequent PRs.
